response:
D

The work done is the same for both students because they lift the same total mass (same weight) over the same vertical distance (to the third floor). Work = mgh, which depends only on mass, gravity, and height—not on time. Therefore, options A and B are incorrect; the total work is equal.

Power is the rate of doing work: Power = Work / Time. Since the work is the same but Mary takes less time (30 s vs. 45 s), Mary has the greater power output. This makes option D correct and option C incorrect.
